preamble.title: iohk/programmable-tokens
preamble.description: Aiken implementation of CIP-113 programmable tokens (migrated from Plutarch)

cardano/address/Credential.title: Credential
cardano/address/Credential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cardano/transaction/OutputReference.title: OutputReference
cardano/transaction/OutputReference.description:
An `OutputReference` is a unique reference to an output on-chain. The `output_index`
 corresponds to the position in the output list of the transaction (identified by its id)
 that produced that output

types/ProgrammableLogicGlobalRedeemer.title: ProgrammableLogicGlobalRedeemer
types/ProgrammableLogicGlobalRedeemer.description: Redeemer for the global programmable logic stake validator

anyOf.title: TransferAct
anyOf.description: Transfer action with proofs for each token type

anyOf.title: ThirdPartyAct
anyOf.description:
Third party action for admin operations (seizure, freeze, etc.)
 Supports multiple UTxOs in a single transaction

fields.title: registry_node_idx
fields.description: Index of the registry node in reference inputs

fields.title: outputs_start_idx
fields.description: Starting index in outputs where processed outputs begin

types/RegistrationMode.title: RegistrationMode
types/RegistrationMode.description:
Selects which registration flow `RegistryInsert` is exercising.

 * `RegisterOnly` — the substandard authorises the registration but no
   programmable tokens of the new policy are minted. Useful for
   real-world-asset issuance, supply-aware substandards that need to
   initialise global state before any token exists, or any scenario
   where minting cannot happen at registration time.

 * `RegisterAndMint` — registration AND a first mint of the new policy
   happen in the same transaction. Current behaviour, now explicit.

 In both modes the substandard's `minting_logic_script` MUST appear in
 `tx.withdrawals` (proof of instance — the substandard authorises the
 registration). The modes differ only in the `tx.mint` shape:
   * RegisterOnly  ⇒ no entries under the new policy in `tx.mint`.
   * RegisterAndMint ⇒ `tx.mint` contains an entry under the new
     policy. Quantity sign is not checked on-chain: burn-only of a
     brand-new policy is ledger-impossible (no prior tokens exist
     before registration), so the first mint is positive by
     construction.

types/RegistryProof.title: RegistryProof
types/RegistryProof.description: Proof that a token is (or is not) in the registry

anyOf.title: TokenExists
anyOf.description: Token exists in the registry at this reference input index

anyOf.title: TokenDoesNotExist
anyOf.description: Token does not exist (proof via covering node at this index)

types/RegistryRedeemer.title: RegistryRedeemer
types/RegistryRedeemer.description: Redeemer for registry minting policy (linked list operations)

anyOf.title: RegistryInit
anyOf.description: Initialize the registry with the origin node

anyOf.title: RegistryInsert
anyOf.description:
Insert a new programmable token policy into the registry.

 `minting_logic_script` is the credential of the substandard's
 withdraw-0 minting logic. registry_mint enforces that:
   * the new node's datum field `minting_logic_script` equals this
     value (datum-matches-redeemer check, prevents the registrar
     from writing a different credential into the datum than they
     claim in the redeemer);
   * the issuance template parameterised with this credential hashes
     to `key` (cryptographic binding: the `hashed_param` used in the
     policy id derivation is the inner 28-byte hash of this
     credential — derived inside the validator, NOT supplied as a
     separate redeemer field);
   * the substandard's withdraw-0 logic is invoked
     (`minting_logic_script` in `tx.withdrawals`) regardless of mode.

 `mode` selects between RegisterOnly (no programmable-token mint
 allowed) and RegisterAndMint (registration + first mint, current
 behaviour).

fields.title: minting_logic_cred
fields.description: The credential of the minting logic script that must be invoked
